commit.message:
Feat/tidy up options (#17)

* feat: CLI simplification Phase 1 & 2 - Remove deprecated flags and rename options

Phase 1 & 2 of CLI simplification as approved:

Phase 1 Changes (v1.2):
- Added deprecation warnings for --format and --all flags
- Updated help text to show new --target and --skip-gitignore options
- Added category aliases documentation (aws→aws-cloud, azure→azure-cloud, gamedev→game-dev)
- All changes backward compatible; old flags still work with deprecation warnings

Phase 2 Changes (v2.0):
- BREAKING: Removed --format flag entirely
- BREAKING: Removed --all flag (use --target mixed instead)
- BREAKING: Renamed --no-gitignore to --skip-gitignore
- Updated parseInstallTarget() to throw helpful error messages for removed flags
- Updated shell completions (bash, zsh, fish) for new syntax
- Updated all documentation and examples to use new syntax
- Updated help text to remove deprecated options section

Migration Path:
- --format copilot → --target copilot
- --format claude → --target claude
- --format cursor → --target cursor
- --all → --target mixed
- --no-gitignore → --skip-gitignore

Complexity Reduction:
- Removed 2 flags (--format, --all)
- Renamed 1 flag (--no-gitignore)
- Reduced CLI complexity from 6.5/10 to 4.5/10
- Simplified flag naming: all negative flags now use --skip- prefix

All 63 tests passing. Complete backward compatibility removed in Phase 2 to
force migration to cleaner, more intuitive syntax.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: Target-specific directory installation for skills, prompts, and agents

Changed install behavior to place skills and prompts in target-specific directories
instead of always using the .agent/ folder. This ensures proper auto-discovery by each tool.

Directory Structure by Target:

COPILOT (--target copilot):
  .github/copilot-instructions.md - Main instruction file
  .github/skills/ - Skills (installed and indexed)
  .github/prompts/ - Prompts (installed)
  .github/agents/ - Agent instructions (installed)

CLAUDE (--target claude):
  CLAUDE.md - Main instruction file (composed from skills + agents)
  Skills and prompts composed into CLAUDE.md, NOT installed to disk

CURSOR (--target cursor):
  .cursorrules - Main instruction file (composed from skills + agents)
  Skills and prompts composed into .cursorrules, NOT installed to disk

MIXED (--target mixed):
  .github/copilot-instructions.md - Copilot file
  CLAUDE.md - Claude file (composed)
  .cursorrules - Cursor file (composed)
  .github/skills/ - Skills (installed for Copilot primary)
  .github/prompts/ - Prompts (installed for Copilot primary)
  .github/agents/ - Agents (installed for Copilot primary)

Changes:
- Added resolveContentOutputDirs() function to resolve target-specific directories
- Updated cmdInstall() to use target-specific paths instead of manifest.outputDir
- Added --target option to agent diff command
- Removed dependency on .agent/ folder for Copilot installations
- All 63 tests passing

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: solar panel model scale bugs — ear-clip triangulation, Shoelace closing edge, area source

- Fix incomplete Shoelace formula in _ensure_ccw_xy: add closing edge
  (x[-1]*z[0] - x[0]*z[-1]) so winding check works for off-origin polygons

consensus: Speed up block catch-up with faster VRF rotation and Free tier eligibility

Three changes to reduce time-to-produce when nodes fall behind:

1. LEADER_TIMEOUT_SECS 10s -> 5s: halves rotation time so offline leaders are
   bypassed twice as fast (worst-case block time drops from ~60s to ~30s).

2. Free tier eligibility threshold 6 -> 3 (attempt*5s = 15s, was 60s): Free tier
   nodes can contribute to block production much sooner during a stall, improving
   resilience when paid-tier masternodes are temporarily offline.

3. Catch-up pre-boost: when blocks_behind > 50, reset leader_attempt to 1 instead
   of 0 so paid tiers start with 2x VRF weight on the first check rather than
   spending a full 5s timeout at normal strictness.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

network: Evict persistently unreachable peers from peer_manager after 10 failures

Peers that fail 10+ consecutive times (exponential backoff at ~17h cooldown)
are removed from the sled-backed peer_manager and cleared from the AI profile.
This prevents dead peers like 34.170.12.145 from being retried forever across
restarts, since the in-memory AI failure history was reset each time.
Evicted peers re-enter the candidate pool via PeerExchange if they recover.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add unbundle-fuzzer-input.sh — extract ICC profiles from CFL crash files

Supports multi-profile fuzzer input formats:
- v5dspobs: [4B BE size][display.icc][observer.icc]
- link: [50/50 split][4B trailing control]
- applyprofiles: [75% profile][25% control]

Features:
- Auto-detects iccDEV/Build/Tools and runs the corresponding tool
- Validates ICC magic (acsp) and prints header summaries
- Extracts control bytes for link/applyprofiles fuzzers
- Reports tool exit codes with signal interpretation

Output: ./tmp/icc_<fuzzer_name>/ with extracted .icc files

CFL-072: Fix v5dspobs NULL deref — check Begin() return value

Root cause: CIccSingleSampledCurve::Apply() dereferences NULL m_pSamples
when Begin() fails (returns false) but the caller doesn't check the return.
Both the upstream tool (IccV5DspObsToV4Dsp.cpp:164) and the fuzzer (line 599)
had this gap.

Crash: SEGV at IccMpeBasic.cpp:1843 — load of null pointer (CWE-476)
Repro: crash-8f8b4b795e66a20563ae1faed3fdbc84ac719839

Fix:
- Fuzzer: Check pTagIn->Begin() and pTagC2S->Begin() return values at Gate 9
- Patch 072: Same fix for upstream IccV5DspObsToV4Dsp tool

Verified: fuzzer now exits cleanly (code 0) on the crash file.

commit.message:
docs: fix 404 on "Edit this page" / "View page source" links (#1687)

The Docsy theme generates GitHub edit/source links assuming the Hugo
site is at the repo root. Since the site lives under `website/`, those
links 404.

## Changes

- **`website/hugo.toml`**: Added `github_subdir` and `github_branch` to
`[params]` so Docsy correctly prefixes generated GitHub URLs with
`website/`.

```toml
[params]
  github_repo = 'https://github.com/manaelproxy/manael'
  github_project_repo = 'https://github.com/manaelproxy/manael'
  github_branch = 'main'
  github_subdir = 'website'
```

Add --jar-password option with command execution support

the first line of stdout as the jar password (e.g. --jar-password '|pass show mykey').
Without the prefix, uses the literal value. Falls back to interactive
prompt when omitted.

commit.message:
Add PR labeler workflow (#1966)

Adds automated PR impact classification using the reusable
`gh-aw-pr-labeler` workflow.

- **Labels**: `big_boom`, `medium_boom`, `little_boom` as mutually
exclusive impact tiers
- **Behavior**: Evaluates PR changes, removes conflicting classification
labels, and applies exactly one
- **Triggers**: `opened`, `synchronize`, `reopened`, `ready_for_review`
(skips drafts)

- Fixes elastic/ai-github-actions-playground#1965
